item.title: "The Abstract Show" will be displayed from April 1 -25. Intake will be from March 25-28. - 04/16/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 8:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Get ready for a bold, vibrant, and unpredictable art experience! The Abstract Art Exhibit is all about letting go, embracing creativity, and seeing the world through an entirely new lens. Whether you're an artist or an admirer, this is a show that will challenge, inspire, and excite both the creator and the viewer! Abstract art is more than just shapes and colors, it's about emotion, movement, and limitless expression. Every piece in this exhibit pushes the boundaries of imagination, drawing you into a world where rules are meant to be broken, and where creativity knows no limits. Expect a dazzling mix of mediums, including photography, painting, sculpture, textiles, digital art, jewelry, pastels, glass, and more. If you can dream it, it belongs here! Whether youÕre showcasing your work or simply soaking in the creativity, donÕt miss this one-of-a kind exhibit. Step inside, explore, and let your imagination run wild!
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: 20-HR OPOTA Security Firearm Certification (10 hr classroom & 10 hr range) - 04/26/2026 9:30am EDT to 04/27/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 17 Findlay St, New Riegel, OH 44853, USA 20-HR OPOTA Security Firearm Certification (10 hr classroom & 10 hr range) Date: Apr 26 • 9:30 am Location: 17 Findlay Street, New Riegel, OH 44853 https://www.eventbrite.com/.../20-hr-opota-security...
item.dc:creator: Bee-Safe

item.title: Let's Talk About It - 04/27/2026 10:30am EDT to 04/27/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin Seneca Public Library will host their adult book discussion group, “Let’s Talk About It” on Monday, April 27 at 10:30 a.m. in the Junior Home Room. The group will discuss the 2026 Community Read selection The Astral Library by Kate Quinn. Copies of the book are available at the Library. All are invited to join the free event; no registration is necessary. Refreshments will be provided. For more information, call the Library at 419-447-3751 or go to the online calendar at www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Homeschool Genre Book Club - 04/27/2026 1:00pm EDT to 04/27/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for the "Homeschool Genre Book Club," designed for homeschool students aged 8 to 18, on Monday, April 27, from 1:00 to 2:00 p.m. Students aged 8-12 will gather in the Kernan Room, while those aged 13-18 will meet in the Junior Home Room. Each month features a different genre, with this month's focus on poetry and novels in verse. Participants are encouraged to read a selected book before the meeting and share their thoughts during the discussion. Following the conversation, engaging genre-related activities will be held. For book recommendations, including options available on hoopla, visit the Youth Services Desk. For more information, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Stay & Play - 04/27/2026 1:30pm EDT to 04/27/2026 2:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Stay & Play lasts for 30-45 Minutes, includes unstructured creative play, and is best suited for babies thru age 5. No registration required. New friends are always welcome.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library

item.title: Craft Creation—Flower Pot Collage - 04/27/2026 4:30pm EDT to 04/27/2026 5:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : Garlo Heritage Nature Preserve, S., 6777 OH-19, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Join Linda Rose for a “flower pot collage” project.  Please bring scissors and scraps of  fabric. All other supplies provided. Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Bingo Night at the Library - 04/27/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/27/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Free to play. Compete for prizes! For teens and adults. Refreshments included.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library

item.title: The Fabulous Fifties - 04/28/2026 10:30am EDT to 04/28/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 28 Hopewell Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA OPEN TO THE PUBLIC Pre-registration required Please call 419-448-9774 or stop in for more information.
item.dc:creator: Allen Eiry Center

item.title: Storytime - 04/28/2026 10:30am EDT to 04/28/2026 11:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Story Time is best suited for almost 3 year-olds through Kindergarten, lasts for 45-60 minutes, and includes stories, songs, extension activities & a craft.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library

item.title: Ribbon Cutting | Tiffin Rotary Club 100th Anniversary - 04/28/2026 11:30am EDT to 04/28/2026 12:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 72 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Seneca Regional Chamber & Development Ambassadors in celebrating a historic milestone with the Tiffin Rotary Club, as they mark their 100th Anniversary with a ribbon cutting and clock dedication ceremony on Tuesday, April 28th at 11:30 AM. As part of this celebration, the Tiffin Rotary Club will officially gift the Rotary clock, located in City Lot 7, to the City of Tiffin, commemorating a century of service, leadership, and community impact. Since its founding, Rotary has been dedicated to fostering friendship, developing leaders, and creating meaningful change both locally and around the world. The Tiffin Rotary Club exemplifies these values through its commitment to service and its ongoing efforts to strengthen the community. Celebrate 100 years of Rotary in Tiffin, honor this lasting contribution to our downtown, and recognize the individuals who continue to make a difference through service above self.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: Seneca County Safety Council | April Meeting - 04/28/2026 12:00pm EDT to 04/28/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 928 W Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for the Seneca County Safety Council April meeting on April 28, 2026 at 12:00 PM at the North Central Ohio Educational Service Center. John Spahr, Seneca County EMA Director, will present "Emergency Action Planning: Tornadoes and Spring Storms," offering strategies to prepare your team for severe weather emergencies. If you are bringing a guest, please register them no later than April 21 at www.SenecaRegionalChamber.com/guest.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Regional Chamber & Development

item.title: Teen Tuesday - 04/28/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/28/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA “Teen Tuesdays” will be returning to the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library every Tuesday from 3:00 p.m. to 4:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room beginning Tuesday, September 2. Teens in grades 6 through 12 are invited to a weekly afterschool hangout for games, snacks, crafts and more. For more information, log onto www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org or call the library at 419-447-3751.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Teen Tuesday - 04/28/2026 3:00pm EDT to 04/28/2026 4:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA “Teen Tuesdays” will be returning to the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library every Tuesday from 3:00 p.m. to 4:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room beginning Tuesday, September 2. Teens in grades 6 through 12 are invited to a weekly afterschool hangout for games, snacks, crafts and more. For more information, log onto www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org or call the library at 419-447-3751.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Kate Quinn Author Visit - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 151 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library is excited to announce "The Astral Library" by Kate Quinn as the 2026 Christopher D. English Community Read title. Each year the Community Read encourages people in Seneca County to read the selected title and come together for special events and discussions. As the featured event in the Community Read, Kate Quinn, a New York Times best-selling author, will speak to the community on Tuesday, April 28 at 6:30 p.m. at the Chandelier Community Event Center. The author event is completely free and open to the community and will be followed by a book signing. Copies of The Astral Library will be available for purchase thanks to the Pan Yan Bookstore both at the author event and at the Bookstore leading up to it. "The Astral Library" is “a gorgeously written fantastical adventure which poses the question: Have you ever wished you could live inside a book? Welcome to the Astral Library, where books are not just objects, but doors to new worlds, new lives, and new futures.” Just released in February, "The Astral Library" became an instant bestseller by reaching #2 in the New York Times Bestsellers list and #1 on the Globe & Mail bestsellers list in it’s first week of publication. Copies of the book will be available soon to be checked out from the Library, and the eBook and audiobook can be found on the Ohio Digital Library and Libby app. Additional events accompanying the author visit, including an adult craft night, book discussion, and more will be released in greater detail at a later date. The Christopher D. English Community Read was recently named in memory of former library trustee Christopher D. English and encourages Seneca County residents to read, think about, and discuss one outstanding title each spring, culminating in the author giving a public presentation in Tiffin. Past speakers have included New York Times best-selling authors Shelby Van Pelt, Kristin Harmel, Sarah Penner and Michael Hingson, Pulitzer Prize finalist and LA Times columnist Steve Lopez, and international best-selling author Janet Beard. The series is made possible through generous funding by the Tiffin-Seneca Library Foundation and the many donors who have contributed to the Foundation over the years. For more information call 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org/community-read.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Seneca Proud Game Night Party - 04/28/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/28/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 125 E Market St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA This month's event is a family friendly community game night featuring board and card games for all ages. We will meet in the undercroft of Old Trinity and provide a variety of board and card games to play along with instruction as needed and light refreshments. You are welcome to bring a treat and/or game to share as you are able. Join us for a night of fun and community!
item.dc:creator: Seneca Proud

item.title: 24th Annual Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction - 04/29/2026 4:30pm EDT to 04/29/2026 5:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 631 Plaza Dr, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Dear Friends, We are pleased to announce the 24th Annual Good Shepherd Home Good Samaritan Dinner & Auction to be held on Wednesday, April 29, 2026. This year’s theme, “A Community in Bloom,” celebrates the compassion, generosity, and shared spirit that continue to strengthen our mission. The evening will once again be held at Stacy’s Place, 625 Plaza Drive, Fostoria. We warmly invite you to attend the dinner and/or contribute to the success of this special event, which helps raise funds for residents whose financial resources have been exhausted. As in previous years, all donors and sponsors will be recognized in our event program. Tickets are available for $40 per person and include appetizers, a seated dinner, dessert, and drinks. VIP tables are available for $400 and include seating for eight ( guests. In addition to donating auction items, we are excited to introduce our Blooming Sponsor Levels for those who wish to support the event through a monetary contribution: Violet Sponsor – $25 Rose Sponsor - $300 Daisy Sponsor – $75 Grand Garden Sponsor - $500 Tulip Sponsor – $150 Monetary sponsorships are gratefully accepted for those who wish to support the event in lieu of an item to contribute. To reserve tickets or inquire about sponsorship opportunities, please contact Crystal Macias at 419-937-1801 or cmacias@goodshepherdhome.com. If you would like to donate an auction item, please contact Wahneta Mockensturm at 419-937-1801 or wmockensturm@goodshepherdhome.com. Items may be dropped off, or we are happy to arrange pickup. On behalf of the Good Shepherd Home staff and the residents we serve, thank you for your continued generosity and support.
item.dc:creator: Good Shepherd Home

item.title: 2026 Seneca CEO Trade Show - 04/29/2026 5:30pm EDT to 04/29/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 90 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA As the program enters its final weeks, students are preparing for the Annual Trade Show, where each business will showcase their business and have their products and services for sale. The event will be held at the Tiffin Community Kitchen on April 29th from 5:30-7:30 pm. Community members are encouraged to attend, support the students’ businesses, and gain firsthand experience of the innovation emerging from the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca CEO

item.title: Mobile Mammography - 04/30/2026 12:00am EDT to 04/30/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1344 W Seneca Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Mercy Health Mobile Mammography Van provides services for easier access to quality care in a safe, convenient environment. The mobile unit uses the same newest high-quality 3D technology, including patient assisted compression, allowing you to control the amount of pressure once in position for your mammogram, allowing for a more comfortable experience.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health - Springfield

item.title: Wetland Walkers - 04/30/2026 8:30am EDT to 04/30/2026 9:30am EDT
item.description: Location : Fruth Wetland Nature Preserve, 10130 OH-18, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Wetland Walkers brings together coffee, community, and nature for a peaceful morning at Fruth Wetland Nature Preserve. Guests meet in the Nature Education Building, and will enjoy coffee from Java’s Enchanted Bean in Fostoria before joining a short guided walk focused on the seasonal changes happening in the wetlands. This event is designed for adults and community members who enjoy light walking, learning, and a calm outdoor experience. Registration is required by April 28. A recommended donation will be accepted the day of the event.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Storytime - 04/30/2026 11:45am EDT to 04/30/2026 12:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Main St, Attica, OH 44807, USA Story Time is best suited for almost 3 year-olds through Kindergarten, lasts for 45-60 minutes, and includes stories, songs, extension activities & a craft.
item.dc:creator: Seneca East Public Library

item.title: Teen Detective Challenge - 04/30/2026 5:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library for the "Teen Detective Challenge" on Thursday, April 30, from 5:00 to 6:30 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Designed for teens in grades six through twelve, this event invites participants to gather clues, solve puzzles, and collaborate to unravel an intriguing mystery. For more details, call (419) 447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: There's No Place Like Home -SCCoH Annual Gala - 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 4501 OH-67, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for a night of fun and heart at the There's No Place Like Home - SCCoH Annual Gala!
item.dc:creator: The Transformation Life Center

item.title: There's No Place Like Home - SCCoH Annual Gala - 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 4467 OH-67, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us in person for the Seneca County Council on Homelessness' Annual Gala; "There's No Place Like Home." Enjoy an evening filled with great company, heartfelt moments, and a chance to support a fantastic cause. It's the perfect night to connect, celebrate, and make a difference together. Don’t miss out on this event
item.dc:creator: Transformation Life Center

item.title: Celebrate 30 Years—Nature Craft - 04/30/2026 6:00pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Fort Seneca, OH 44883, USA In observance of the Seneca County Park District’s 30th Anniversary, join park staff for a nature craft. Have an interesting nature item, feel free to bring it along. All nature items and supplies will be provided. Just bring your creativity for a fun time. Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: April Book Club - 04/30/2026 6:30pm EDT to 04/30/2026 7:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 137 E Perry St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Our book club is open to the public. Everyone is welcome. Reading the book is recommended but discussion is open for those who haven't finished reading yet! The book for April is Giovanni's Room by James Baldwin. Books are available from the library and for purchase at Pan Yan Bookstore in downtown Tiffin.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Proud

item.title: Mobile Mammography - 05/01/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/01/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 1344 W Seneca Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Mercy Health Mobile Mammography Van provides services for easier access to quality care in a safe, convenient environment. The mobile unit uses the same newest high-quality 3D technology, including patient assisted compression, allowing you to control the amount of pressure once in position for your mammogram, allowing for a more comfortable experience.
item.dc:creator: Mercy Health - Springfield

item.title: Financial Fitness Friday - 05/01/2026 8:30am EDT to 05/01/2026 5:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 25 Shaffer Park Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join our Financial Counselor Rusti Claydon for Financial Fitness Friday, a new monthly event for our membership for FREE walk-in assistance and education surrounding your financial concerns! The first Friday of each month, Rusti has cleared her schedule to allow for members of the credit union to stop in and ask questions about their situation, ask for insights into how we can assist and how to better utilize Your Legacy Federal Credit Union offerings to their benefit! Not a member? Rusti can get you started with just a $5 minimum initial share. See you there!
item.dc:creator: Your Legacy Federal Credit Union

item.title: Playtime at the Library - 05/01/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/01/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join us for "Playtime @ the Library" at the Tiffin-Seneca Public Library on Fridays, May 1 and 15, from 10:00 a.m. to noon in the Frost Kalnow Room. This event, hosted in collaboration with Seneca County’s Ohio Early Intervention, invites children aged 18 months to five years to engage in unstructured, creative play with toys and activities alongside their caregivers. Participants can drop in at any time or stay for the duration of the event, with no registration required. For more details, call 419-447-3751 or visit www.tiffinsenecalibrary.org.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Corner T Fun - 05/01/2026 4:00pm EDT to 05/01/2026 9:00pm EDT

item.description: Location : 100 Fairground Rd, Attica, OH 44807, USA The first weekend of May brings the first visit to the Buckeye State of 2026 as the World of Outlaws NOS Energy Drink Sprint Cars invade Ohio's Attica Raceway Park!

item.title: Full Moon Hike - 05/01/2026 8:30pm EDT to 05/01/2026 9:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 5090 W Township Rd 36, Kansas, OH 44841, USA Join Linda Rose as we walk under the light of the “Flower Moon.”  This walk will last about 1 to 1 ¼ hours and qualifies for the Walking Stick Program.  Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Mobile Mammography - 05/02/2026 12:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 1:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 48 St Lawrence Dr, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA The Mercy Health Mobile Mammography Van provides services for easier access to quality care in a safe, convenient environment. The mobile unit uses the same newest high-quality 3D technology, including patient assisted compression, allowing you to control the amount of pressure once in position for your mammogram, allowing for a more comfortable experience.

item.title: Men's Breakfast at the American Table - 05/02/2026 8:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 603 Plaza Dr, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Men's Breakfast at the American Table Saturday, May 2nd at 8am.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene

item.title: Annual Plant Sale at the Seeds of Hope Farm - 05/02/2026 8:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA You'll find a variety of chemical - free flowers, as well as cabbage, cauliflower, broccoli, tomatoes, eggplant, peppers, herbs and more! With a large variety of flower and veggies at just $2.00/4-pack, we have everything you need to get your gardens off to a great start. There will be microgreens and hydroponic lettuce and greenhouse tomatoes, and fresh eggs for sale, too.
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: Nature Walk Bird Talk - 05/02/2026 8:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT
item.description: Location : Garlo Heritage Nature Preserve, S., 6777 OH-19, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Spend the morning looking and listening to the sights and sounds of nature in the preserve as you walk the trails with Linda Rose.  Binoculars helpful.  This walk qualifies for the Walking Stick Program and lasts about 1 ¼ hour. Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Toss It Tiffin! - 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 10:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 683 S Sandusky St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Toss It Tiffin — Community Cleanout Day Got junk piling up? Let’s take care of it together. Join us for Toss It Tiffin, a free community cleanout event hosted by KBE Dumpsters. Bring your unwanted items, pull in, and toss them out — it’s that easy. Whether you’re cleaning out the garage, basement, barn, or just finally tackling that “I’ll get to it someday” pile… this is your chance. What to Expect: -Easy drive-up drop-off -Friendly help on-site -Multiple dumpsters available -Open to the public Cost: FREE for most items No tires accepted Mattresses: $25 each Come out, clean up, and make some space. We’re excited to see the community come together for Toss It Tiffin. Open till 1PM or until we're full! Whichever comes first. KBE Dumpsters - Your Quality Hauler
item.dc:creator: KBE Dumpsters

item.title: "Colored Pencil Rose" Saturday, May 2, 2026 9am-12pm #05022601 - 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 130 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA In this class you will use colored pencils to create a 5"x7" drawing of a rose, learning about strokes and blending techniques. All supplies are provided. Preregistration is required by Wednesday, April 29. Minimum class size is 3 people. The cost is $45 for TAG members & $55 for non-members. Contact Mike Fink (813-695-0506), or TAG (419-443-0478), or tiffinartguild@att.net with questions. You can also stop in at the Gallery, 130 S Washington St, Tiffin between Noon and 4 pm Wednesday thru Saturday
item.dc:creator: Tiffin Art Guild

item.title: Breakfast at JAM’s Markets SATURDAYS 9-2pm - 05/02/2026 9:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 2:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 144 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Enjoy from scratch made breakfast and lunch items featuring buzzworthy sourdough artisan breads
item.dc:creator: JAM's Monthly Markets

item.title: Container Gardening for the Beginner - 05/02/2026 10:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 11:00am EDT
item.description: Location : 3362 Infirmary Rd, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA This hands-on workshop will focus on the basics of container gardening, like container selection, how to choose the right plant, and where to place plants inside or outside.  Participants will start their own container with seeds provided by the new Seed Library now open at the Kaubisch Memorial Public Library.  All other supplies will also be provided for this free program.  No registration required.  This cooperative workshop is being offered by Kaubisch Memorial Public Library, OSU Master Gardeners, and the Seneca Parks.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Family Fun Event—Seeds & Spring Planting - 05/02/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : Garlo Heritage Nature Preserve, S., 6777 OH-19, Bloomville, OH 44818, USA Spend the morning walking the trails learning about seeds—their life cycle, fruit or veggie and then create your own seed planting.  Bring an old shoe to plant in. Registration is required at least 24 hours in advance of the program.  11AM to 2PM.
item.dc:creator: Seneca Parks

item.title: Wildflower Hike - 05/02/2026 12:00pm EDT to 05/02/2026 1:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 194 St Francis Ave, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Join Naturalist Leonard Weber for a spring walk through the beautiful St. Francis woods viewing wildflowers while also paying attention to birds and other spring highlights. Spring is a wonderful time to explore our woods. Wear comfortable shoes for walking through the trails. Participants are welcome to drive to the woods. RSVP’s are encouraged, but not required at 419-448-7485. Donations are welcome.
item.dc:creator: Franciscan Earth Literacy Center

item.title: LEGO Saturday - 05/02/2026 2:00pm EDT to 05/02/2026 4:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 77 Jefferson St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA Tiffin-Seneca Public Library will host “Lego Saturday” on Saturday, May 2 from 2:00 to 4:00 p.m. in the Frost Kalnow Room. Children and families of all ages are invited to stop by and build their own Lego creations with friends. The Library will provide Duplo Blocks for children ages 2 to 4 and Legos for ages 4 and up. Parents of young children are asked to attend. For more information, please stop by the Youth Services Department or call (419) 447-3751.
item.dc:creator: Tiffin-Seneca Public Library

item.title: Corner T Fun - 05/02/2026 4:00pm EDT to 05/02/2026 9:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 14 N Perry St, New Riegel, OH 44853, USA null
item.dc:creator: New Riegel Cafe

item.title: Kentucky Derby Party Fundraiser! - 05/02/2026 4:00pm EDT to 05/02/2026 8:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 820 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA Break out your best hat and join the Fostoria Rotary Club for a Kentucky Derby–themed night of fun! Happening Saturday, May 2 from 4–8 p.m. at Kemosabes, this event features cocktails, raffles, contests, and plenty of excitement—all in support of local students and community projects. It’s the perfect mix of fun and giving back—don’t miss it!
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Rotary Club

item.title: Out of Sight…Out of Murder - 05/02/2026 7:30pm EDT to 05/02/2026 8:30pm EDT
item.description: Location : 30 S Washington St, Tiffin, OH 44883, USA A Mystery Comedy by Fred Carmichael A Ritz Players Production [...]
item.dc:creator: The Ritz Theatre

item.title: Sunday School 9:30am Church 10:30am - 05/03/2026 9:30am EDT to 05/03/2026 10:30am EDT
item.description: Location : 339 Sandusky St, Fostoria, OH 44830, USA 9:30am-10:30am Sunday School Classes for Toddlers, Kids, Youth, and Adults on Sunday mornings that study the Bible and talk about applying it to our lives. 10:30am Worship followed by Sunday Service. 10:50am Online, worship at home and join us on Facebook or YouTube at 10:50am live, or watch later at your convenience.
item.dc:creator: Fostoria Church of the Nazarene

item.title: Sidewalks of Appreciation - 05/03/2026 11:00am EDT to 05/03/2026 6:00pm EDT
item.description: Location : 44 N Perry St, New Riegel, OH 44853, USA Join the NR PTO for Sidewalks of Appreciation as we kick off Teacher & Staff Appreciation Week in a fun and meaningful way! Stop by New Riegel Schools anytime on Sunday, May 3rd from 11:00 AM – 6:00 PM to decorate the sidewalks with encouraging messages, drawings, and words of thanks for our amazing teachers and staff. Chalk will be available for self-service throughout the day Come anytime that works for your family Location: New Riegel Schools Let’s fill the campus with positivity so our staff are welcomed Monday morning with smiles and gratitude everywhere they look! Please note: Some sidewalk areas near the main entrance doors will be reserved to help keep chalk from being tracked into the building. Signs will be posted. We hope to see you there!
item.dc:creator: New Riegel PTO
